contract_claim: Arguments are joined into a single string via `[string]::Join(' ', ...)` and assigned to `$psi.Arguments`. For ProcessStartInfo, `Arguments` is a single string passed directly to the process, which means multi-word arguments with complex quoting may not be correctly parsed by all target executables (especially those that don't use standard Windows command-line parsing). In particular, pacman commands at L147 pass a multi-token bash `-lc` argument that relies on this quoting.
observed_behavior: Arguments are joined into a single string via `[string]::Join(' ', ...)` and assigned to `$psi.Arguments`. For ProcessStartInfo, `Arguments` is a single string passed directly to the process, which means multi-word arguments with complex quoting may not be correctly parsed by all target executables (especially those that don't use standard Windows command-line parsing). In particular, pacman commands at L147 pass a multi-token bash `-lc` argument that relies on this quoting.
